title: poa bolanderi var. howellii
intro: Poa bolanderi var. howellii, also known as Howell's blue grass, is a rare and unique variety of the Poa bolanderi species, a type of grass native to California in the United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been found to have healing effects on wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Poa bolanderi var. howellii include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and even exhibit antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made through infusions or decoctions, and it can be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal uses.
